headline: Work isn't just tasks. It's who you are.
subtitle: THE PROFESSIONAL SELF
body: For most professionals, their job title isn't just a description of what they do — it's a core part of how they see themselves. The writer. The strategist. The analyst. Strip that away, and you're not removing a workflow. You're removing a self.

headline: AI doesn't feel like a tool. It feels like a threat.
subtitle: IDENTITY UNDER THREAT
body: When AI can replicate your signature skill — the thing you spent years getting good at — it doesn't arrive as a helpful upgrade. It arrives like an unnervingly capable intern who has already learned your favourite trick. The unease is rational.

headline: Think of retirement. Now make it involuntary.
subtitle: THE RETIREMENT ANALOGY
body: Retirement research tells us that losing a role you've anchored your identity to is genuinely destabilising — even when the exit is chosen. Now imagine that same destabilisation, unasked for, mid-career. That's the psychological territory AI is walking into.

headline: The real question workers are asking.
subtitle: THE QUIETER QUESTION
body: It's not "will I still have a job?" It's quieter and harder: "If I use this, am I still the writer? The strategist? The expert?" Training programmes don't answer that question. Neither does a productivity deck from leadership.

headline: Organisations are framing this all wrong.
subtitle: THE EFFICIENCY TRAP
body: "AI will make us more efficient" lands as: we need fewer of you. The defensive resistance most companies are seeing isn't obstinance. It's a perfectly sane response to a poorly made case.

headline: The fix: sparring partner, not rival.
subtitle: A BETTER FRAMING
body: The framing that actually works is the rehearsal room — a place where you sharpen your thinking, test ideas, move faster. The AI doesn't replace the writer; the writer uses it to write better. That's not spin. It's a genuinely different relationship with the tool.

headline: Help people build a new story of themselves.
subtitle: THE REAL GOAL
body: The goal isn't adoption metrics. It's helping people reach a point where using AI and being themselves are no longer in conflict. That requires change management that takes identity seriously, not just capability.
